mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 1.5% and inflation (CPIAUCSL) is rising month-over-month, the Fed is in active tightening mode. This constrains equity valuations through higher discount rates and reduced corporate profit margins, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:As of 2022-08-15, FEDFUNDS=1.68% and CPIAUCSL=295.271, indicating early tightening cycle
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with persistent inflation
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC between the interaction signal and forward returns falls below 0.05 over any 12-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in total nonfarm payroll employment (PAYEMS) over a 3-month rolling window signals labor market deterioration and recession risk, triggering equity risk-off positioning and negative forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 60% of instances where PAYEMS declines exceed 0.5% over 3 months, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real retail sales (RSAFS) growth decelerates below 2% year-over-year while housing starts (HOUST) fall below 1400 thousand units annualized, indicating weakening consumer demand and housing activity. This dual demand shock produces negative equity returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS YoY growth < 2% AND HOUST < 1400 thousand units
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of triggered instances, or the joint signal's Rank IC falls below 0.06 over any 18-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth remains below 1% month-over-month for two consecutive months, signaling supply-side weakness and potential stagflation. This constrains earnings growth expectations and produces negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days as investors reprrice growth.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month growth < 1% for 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC between INDPRO momentum and forward returns falls below 0.03 over any 12-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The unemployment rate (UNRATE) remains below 4% while wage growth (proxied by PAYEMS growth) accelerates above 0.5% month-over-month, indicating tight labor markets and wage-price spiral risk. This triggers Fed tightening expectations and negative equity returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE=3.5% and PAYEMS=152536.0K as of 2022-07-15, both indicating tight labor market
regime_hint: tight labor market with wage acceleration
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of triggered instances despite tight labor conditions, or the interaction signal's Rank IC < 0.05 over any 18-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is held constant (month-over-month change < 0.1%) while inflation (CPIAUCSL) continues to rise above 0.3% month-over-month, real rates are declining and monetary policy is effectively easing. This supports equity valuations and produces positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS month-over-month change < 0.1% AND CPIAUCSL month-over-month change > 0.3%
evidence_refs: fact:Real rates compressed as nominal rates lag inflation
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of triggered instances, or the interaction signal's Rank IC falls below 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) is positive and accelerating quarter-over-quarter while housing starts (HOUST) exceed 1500 thousand units annualized, indicating robust demand-side momentum. This supports earnings growth and produces positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of periods where both GDPC1 growth is positive and HOUST > 1500K, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declines month-over-month while the unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises above 4%, indicating simultaneous supply contraction and labor market softening. This stagflationary signal produces negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days as investors reprice recession risk.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO=104.3648 and UNRATE=3.5% as of 2022-06-15 and 2022-07-15
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the interaction signal's Rank IC falls below 0.04 over any 12-month rolling window.
